name: Silero VAD
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Silero Team; ONNX/JIT models

auth.notes: Local inference; model downloaded from torch.hub or HuggingFace

performance.notes: ~1ms per audio chunk on CPU; very lightweight (1MB model); real-time

agent_readiness.notes: State-of-the-art Voice Activity Detection (VAD). Detects speech segments in audio. ~1MB model. 1ms/chunk latency. 8000/16000Hz. Language-agnostic. MIT license. Streaming capable. Used as preprocessing step before ASR (Whisper) to segment long audio into speech chunks. Also detects language. ONNX and PyTorch JIT versions. The standard VAD library for ASR pipelines — used by WhisperX and most speech processing frameworks.

gotchas: VAD only — doesn't transcribe speech (use with Whisper for full pipeline)
Performance degrades with very noisy audio
Requires PyTorch (1MB model but torch dependency is large)
best_when: Segmenting long audio before ASR (Whisper preprocessing)
Real-time speech detection for voice assistants
Any speech processing pipeline needing voice detection
avoid_when: When you need transcription (pair with Whisper for that)
When WebRTC VAD's simpler approach suffices
summary: Silero VAD is the standard voice activity detection library for Python speech pipelines. ~1MB model, 1ms/chunk CPU latency, language-agnostic, MIT license. Detects speech segments in audio for preprocessing before Whisper/ASR transcription. Used internally by WhisperX and most production speech pipelines. Essential preprocessing tool for any ASR workflow.
